title: The Best New Bars in Miami
summary: As a gateway between the U.S., the Caribbean, and Latin America, Miami has long been a champion of diverse flavors — rum and agave spirits mingling with notes of citrus and exotic fruits. Recently, vermouth and sherry-based drinks, along with classic Spanish cocktails and…
whatHappening: Miami's bar scene is experiencing a creative renaissance with new establishments featuring vermouth, sherry, and classic Spanish cocktails. The city's mixology community is expanding beyond its traditional rum and agave spirit foundations to embrace European aperitif-based drinks and diverse flavor profiles.
whoInvolved: Eater Miami, the food and drink publication covering the Miami hospitality scene, is documenting these bar openings. The venues themselves represent both established bartenders and new operators capitalizing on Miami's growing cocktail culture and cosmopolitan palate.
whyMatters: Miami's bar evolution reflects broader industry trends toward classic cocktail techniques and European spirits gaining prominence in American drinking culture. This diversification strengthens Miami's reputation as a culinary and hospitality destination, potentially attracting cocktail enthusiasts and tourism revenue while establishing the city as more than just a rum-focused market.
moreContext: Miami's geographic position as a cultural crossroads between North America, the Caribbean, and Latin America has historically shaped its beverage landscape toward tropical and agave-forward drinks. The recent surge in vermouth and sherry-based cocktails mirrors a nationwide movement among serious bartenders rediscovering pre-Prohibition and European aperitif culture. This trend suggests Miami's bar community is maturing beyond signature daiquiris and mojitos to compete with established cocktail capitals like New York and San Francisco. Local operators are capitalizing on Miami's international demographics and growing reputation for upscale hospitality.

title: Where to Drink the Best Coffee in Miami
summary: Coffee and pastries from Panther Coffee. | Panther Coffee Miami has long been known for excellent Cuban coffee, but the city’s specialty coffee scene took a little longer to find its footing. These days, the Magic City boasts an ever-growing collection of standout coffeehouses…
whatHappening: Miami's coffee scene is experiencing a renaissance, evolving beyond its traditional Cuban coffee roots to embrace specialty coffee culture. The city now features a growing collection of standout coffeehouses offering high-quality coffee and pastries alongside classic options.
whoInvolved: Panther Coffee is leading Miami's specialty coffee movement, having established itself as a cornerstone of the city's coffee community. The coffeehouse has transitioned from being known primarily for excellent Cuban coffee to becoming a flagship for the broader specialty coffee trend sweeping the Magic City.
whyMatters: This shift reflects Miami's maturing food and beverage culture, attracting coffee enthusiasts and tourists seeking quality third-wave coffee experiences. The expansion of specialty coffeehouses strengthens Miami's reputation as a culinary destination and creates new economic opportunities in the hospitality sector.
moreContext: Cuban coffee has long been embedded in Miami's identity, particularly in communities with strong Cuban heritage. However, the specialty coffee movement—characterized by single-origin beans, precise brewing methods, and artisanal preparation—arrived later to Miami than to other major U.S. cities. Panther Coffee's success in bridging traditional Cuban coffee culture with modern specialty coffee trends has validated market demand and paved the way for competitors. This evolution mirrors broader trends in major American cities where coffee culture has become increasingly sophisticated and diverse.

title: Eating News in Miami, July 2026
summary: A different kind of tea time -- tipsy tea, an unbeatable offer for mango lovers, new high profile restaurant in Coral Gables, ladies night with all the perks, a new waterfront option for cocktails and delicious comfort food from leading chefs, decadent ice cream and churros,…
whatHappening: Miami's food and beverage scene is buzzing with new dining and drinking options this July 2026, from tipsy tea experiences to waterfront cocktail bars. The city is rolling out fresh restaurant concepts and promotional events that cater to everything from mango enthusiasts to cocktail lovers.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated is covering the dining developments across Miami neighborhoods including Coral Gables, which is welcoming a high-profile new restaurant. The scene involves established chefs launching comfort food concepts alongside emerging venues capitalizing on Miami's growing food culture.
whyMatters: These new venues reflect Miami's evolution as a culinary destination beyond its traditional seafood and Latin cuisine offerings. The promotional events like ladies nights and specialty experiences suggest restaurants are competing aggressively for diners' attention in a crowded market.
moreContext: Miami's restaurant scene has been expanding rapidly, with Coral Gables establishing itself as a premium dining destination. The trend toward experiential dining—combining alcohol with unique tea concepts and chef-driven comfort food—reflects broader consumer interest in Instagram-worthy, multi-sensory dining experiences. Waterfront dining venues continue to be high-demand real estate in Miami, where location and ambiance command premium pricing. The emphasis on specific offerings like mango-focused dishes taps into local ingredient trends and Miami's cultural food preferences.

title: From Casual Lunch to Serious Dinner: Paperfish Sushi Brickell Stepped Up Its Game
summary: Paperfish Sushi Brickell has always been one of my “go to” places to have lunch or happy hour with a friend and they always like it. With all of the sushi restaurants in town you’d ask why Paperfish Sushi? The lovely décor that brightens up your day the moment you walk in,…
whatHappening: Paperfish Sushi Brickell has elevated its restaurant concept from a casual lunch and happy hour destination to a serious dinner establishment. The upgrade maintains the restaurant's signature appealing décor while expanding its appeal to diners seeking more upscale dining experiences.
whoInvolved: Paperfish Sushi Brickell is a sushi restaurant operating in Miami's Brickell neighborhood. The establishment has built a reputation as a reliable spot for casual meals and social gatherings among locals.
whyMatters: The upgrade positions Paperfish to capture a broader customer base willing to spend more on dinner experiences, potentially increasing revenue and market competitiveness. In Miami's crowded sushi restaurant market, this strategic repositioning differentiates the brand and extends its operating hours' profitability.
moreContext: Paperfish Sushi has long relied on its inviting interior design as a key differentiator in a saturated Miami sushi market. The restaurant previously thrived on daytime and early evening casual traffic but faced potential revenue limitations after traditional dinner hours. This evolution reflects a broader trend among casual dining establishments seeking to capture premium evening traffic. The move suggests the restaurant identified untapped demand for upscale sushi dining in the Brickell area among its existing satisfied customer base.

title: New Places to Shop, Eat in the Miami Design District
summary: With the crowds having gone from the Dolce & Gabbana show and many from FIFA, now is a good time to make a visit to the Miami Design District, especially since new shops have opened that are brands that are probably new to you as well as restaurants. Plus, you might come across…

title: PASTA: The Model Miami Restaurant
summary: Very reasonable prices, excellent food across the board, very good service, low noise decibel so you can easily talk, PASTA is a dream restaurant which should serve as a model to others in Miami. I'll even go so far as to say it has the best pasta in Miami. And on top of all…
whatHappening: PASTA restaurant in Miami is being hailed as a model establishment that excels across all key dining dimensions. The venue is earning praise for combining reasonable pricing, excellent food quality, attentive service, and an acoustically pleasant atmosphere.
whoInvolved: PASTA is a Miami-based restaurant that has caught the attention of Miami Curated, a local dining and lifestyle publication. The establishment operates in Miami's competitive restaurant scene where quality and value often come at odds.
whyMatters: PASTA's success demonstrates that restaurants can deliver premium dining experiences without premium prices, while maintaining operational standards that enhance rather than detract from the guest experience. This model could influence how Miami restaurants approach pricing, noise management, and service consistency going forward.
moreContext: Miami's restaurant landscape is known for high costs and often-crowded venues where conversation is difficult due to noise levels. PASTA distinguishes itself by prioritizing acoustic comfort—a frequently overlooked element in dining establishments—while maintaining what reviewers describe as the best pasta offerings in the city. The restaurant's ability to balance affordability with quality food and service execution sets a benchmark for operational excellence in a market where many establishments sacrifice one element to achieve another.

title: A Day in Sunset Harbour Miami
summary: Sunset Harbour Miami is unique. It is one of Miami Beach’s most popular, vibrant, and walkable neighborhoods. Located on the quieter bay side of South Beach, it trades the chaotic tourist energy of Ocean Drive for a chic, wellness-focused, local lifestyle. It packs a big…
whatHappening: Sunset Harbour in Miami Beach is establishing itself as a premier wellness-focused neighborhood that prioritizes local culture over mass tourism. The bayside district offers a chic, walkable alternative to the crowded Ocean Drive scene just blocks away.
whoInvolved: Miami Curated, a local lifestyle publication, is spotlighting Sunset Harbour as a destination. The neighborhood itself comprises local businesses, residents, and entrepreneurs who have cultivated its wellness-oriented community identity.
whyMatters: This shift reflects broader Miami Beach trends toward experiential, health-conscious tourism and authentic neighborhood experiences. For travelers and potential residents, it signals new opportunities to engage with Miami's culture beyond typical tourist attractions, while for local businesses it validates the demand for wellness and lifestyle-focused establishments.
moreContext: Sunset Harbour sits on the bay side of South Beach, positioning it geographically away from the high-energy Ocean Drive corridor that has traditionally defined Miami Beach tourism. The neighborhood has evolved to attract visitors and residents seeking boutique dining, wellness activities, and authentic local interactions rather than nightlife and beach clubs. This mirrors a wider national trend of travelers prioritizing wellness tourism and off-the-beaten-path neighborhood experiences over conventional resort destinations.

title: Taste the Gables is Back with 75 Restaurants
summary: The City of Coral Gables’ popular restaurant month, Taste the Gables, returns this July with its most expansive lineup to date. More than 75 restaurants, ranging from Michelin-recognized destinations to neighborhood favorites and varied cuisines, are participating, showcasing…
whatHappening: Taste the Gables, Coral Gables' signature restaurant month, is returning this July with 75 participating restaurants—the largest lineup in the event's history. The month-long celebration features everything from Michelin-recognized fine dining establishments to casual neighborhood spots across diverse cuisines.
whoInvolved: The City of Coral Gables is organizing the event, partnering with a broad coalition of local restaurants ranging from upscale destinations to everyday favorites throughout the municipality.
whyMatters: The expanded event drives significant foot traffic and spending to local restaurants during a traditionally slower summer month, boosting revenue for participating establishments. For diners, it offers an accessible way to sample high-end and diverse dining options at potentially discounted prices, strengthening community engagement with the local food scene.
moreContext: Taste the Gables has established itself as a marquee annual event for the Coral Gables dining community, similar to Dine Out programs in other major cities. The growth from previous years to 75 restaurants signals strong post-pandemic recovery in the local restaurant sector and increased participation from both established fine dining and emerging casual concepts. Restaurant months typically feature prix-fixe menus at reduced prices, making premium dining more accessible. Coral Gables' diverse culinary landscape—spanning Latin American, Mediterranean, Asian, and contemporary American cuisines—positions the event as a comprehensive showcase of the area's food culture.
